title: Feature/story-level readiness derivation does not exist — feature_gated_by_item / story_gated_by_item play no role in ready/blocked truth (task-level gates shipped)
description: currentState is the sole ready/blocked/next deriver and buckets only the tasks block. Task-level gate awareness SHIPPED: task_gated_by_item folds into blocked + the nextActions topo order (context-sdk.ts:804-836, TASK-065), and the blocked-by relation set is config-declared (state_derivation.blocked_by, partitioned gate-vs-dependency by role_direction). The residual gap: feature_gated_by_item / story_gated_by_item have no readiness deriver at all — no derivation buckets features or stories, so feature/story-level gates remain documentation-only. roadmap-plan.ts orders phases by membership/precedes (no phase_gated_by_item; out of scope).

evidence.reference: shipped task-level gate partition: blocked_by relations classified gate-vs-dependency by role_direction; task_gated_by_item honored in blocked + topo (TASK-065)
currentState buckets only the tasks block — no feature/story readiness derivation exists

impact: Orderings expressed as feature/story gates are invisible to 'what is ready': feature_gated_by_item / story_gated_by_item edges carry no derived consequence anywhere.
proposed_resolution: FEAT-004 (the proper home): the config-declared blocked-by set generalizes beyond tasks — feature/story-level readiness derivation added so gate-awareness covers all kinds by config declaration, not hardcoded to the tasks block. The task-level NOW leg is closed (TASK-065); this gap tracks the feature/story residual only.
